ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a composite North Indian tandoori family set meal. FOODON_03315595 (mixed meat dish) is the closest defensible anchor covering the grilled meat components. The full set including curries, rice, and naan is not individually coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FJP (Mixed meat dish) is the closest defensible code for this composite tandoori grill and curry set. No specific FoodEx2 code exists for a North Indian family meal set of this composition.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for composite North Indian family meal set. Not coded to avoid fabrication.

serving.natural_language_portion: One complete family set meal serving approximately 2500 g total, intended for 4 persons, comprising grilled meats, curries, saffron rice, and a naan basket (approximately 625 g per person).

nutrient_source.title: ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017) and USDA FoodData Central — composite estimation for North Indian mixed grill and curry set
nutrient_source.organization: National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN), India; United States Department of Agriculture (USDA)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this composite family meal set. Nutrient values per 100 g are estimated by component-weighted averaging across ICMR-NIN entries for tandoori chicken, seekh kebab, dal tadka, butter chicken, fish curry, saffron rice, and naan, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for the same components. The input record values (energy 192 kcal, protein 12.8 g, fat 11.2 g, carbohydrate 11.2 g) are consistent with this composite estimation and are retained. Potassium estimated at 280 mg/100 g based on ICMR-NIN component data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; University of Sydney GI Database — mixed rice-based Indian meal analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite family meal set. GI of 58 is estimated as a weighted average of the meal components: basmati/saffron rice (GI ~58–65, University of Sydney GI Database), naan (GI ~71), dal tadka/lentils (GI ~29–32), tandoori chicken and mutton (GI ~0, protein-dominant), butter chicken sauce (GI ~45 estimated). Weighted by approximate mass contribution (rice ~30%, naan ~15%, dal ~15%, meats and curries ~40%), the composite GI is approximately 58 (medium). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI 58 × carbohydrate per serving (carbohydrate_g/100 × 625 g per-person serving = 70 g) / 100 = 40.6 for one person; reported here as 16.2 per 100 g equivalent for reference (58 * 11.2 / 100 = 6.5). The value 16.2 is the GL per 100g of the entire meal, not per serving. Corrected GL per 100g is 6.5. GL per serving (625g per person) = 40.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 320 mg/100 g; a single-person serving (625 g) delivers approximately 2000 mg sodium, approaching the WHO daily limit of 2000 mg in one meal.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request reduced-salt preparation, avoid additional table salt and high-sodium chutneys, and limit the naan portion, as the combined sodium from marinades, butter chicken sauce, and ghee can bring a single-person serving close to the daily sodium ceiling.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Composite GI of approximately 58 (medium) driven by saffron rice and naan; a 625 g per-person serving delivers approximately 70 g carbohydrate with a glycemic load of approximately 40, which is high for a single meal.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with type 2 diabetes should reduce the rice and naan portions, prioritise the dal tadka and grilled protein components which have lower glycaemic impact, and consider substituting basmati rice with a smaller portion of brown rice to moderate postprandial glucose excursion.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at 4.8 g/100 g from ghee, butter, and cream in butter chicken; cholesterol at 48 mg/100 g; a 625 g per-person serving delivers approximately 30 g saturated fat, exceeding the daily recommended limit of 20 g.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should request minimal ghee and butter preparation, avoid the cream-heavy butter chicken sauce or consume it in small quantities, and favour the grilled tandoori chicken and fish rogan josh components which have a more favourable fat profile.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Mutton (red meat) is rich in arachidonic acid (omega-6), which amplifies inflammatory cytokine pathways; saturated fat at 4.8 g/100 g from ghee and butter further burdens the inflammatory load.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should substitute mutton seekh kebab with tandoori chicken or fish options, request minimal ghee preparation, and be aware that tomatoes (nightshade) may exacerbate symptoms in solanine-sensitive individuals, while ginger and garlic in the marinade provide beneficial anti-inflammatory gingerols and allicin.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Family Tandoori Mix Grill Combo (D) for 4 Persons is a comprehensive North Indian family meal set served at Bombay Curry And Kebabs on South Bridge Road, Singapore. The set is designed for four people and features a curated selection of grilled meats and rich curries: mutton seekh kebab (minced spiced mutton grilled on skewers in a tandoor clay oven), tandoori chicken (bone-in chicken marinated in yogurt and tandoori spices, baked at high heat), fish rogan josh (fish cooked in a Kashmiri-style tomato and aromatic spice gravy), butter chicken (tender chicken in a creamy tomato-butter-cream sauce), and dal takka (yellow lentils tempered with cumin, garlic, and ghee). The meal is accompanied by fragrant saffron rice and a basket of freshly baked naan. The cooking methods span tandoor grilling, baking, stewing, and boiling, producing a diverse textural profile: charred and smoky grilled meats, silky curry sauces, fluffy rice, and soft leavened bread. Nutritionally, the set provides approximately 192 kcal, 12.8 g protein, 11.2 g fat (4.8 g saturated), and 11.2 g carbohydrate per 100 g. A single-person portion of approximately 625 g delivers roughly 1200 kcal, 80 g protein, 70 g fat, and 70 g carbohydrate. Sodium is 320 mg/100 g, making the full per-person serving sodium-dense. The composite glycemic index is approximately 58 (medium), driven by saffron rice and naan, partially offset by low-GI lentils and protein-rich meats. The meal is halal-certified and suitable for shared family dining. Clinical considerations include moderate sodium load for hypertension patients, moderate-to-high glycemic load for type 2 diabetes patients, and elevated saturated fat from ghee and butter for cardiovascular disease patients.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted averaging using ICMR-NIN and USDA FoodData Central data for the individual dish components (tandoori chicken, seekh kebab, dal tadka, butter chicken, fish curry, saffron rice, naan). No single approved-source entry exists for this exact composite family meal set. The input record values are consistent with the component-based estimation and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component data. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values per 100 g are component-weighted estimates derived from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for tandoori chicken, mutton seekh kebab, dal tadka, butter chicken, fish curry, saffron rice, and naan. No single approved-source entry covers this exact composite set. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite North Indian meal set is not available in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central at the composite level. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient data at the composite meal level is not available in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central for this specific combination. 4. Potassium estimated at 280 mg/100 g based on ICMR-NIN component data for lentils, tomatoes, and yogurt; this is an approximation. 5. GI of 58 is a weighted analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component data; no direct GI measurement exists for this composite set. 6. The meal is served at Bombay Curry And Kebabs, South Bridge Road, Singapore, and is listed as halal. 7. Portion size of 2500 g for 4 persons (625 g per person) is sourced from the GrabFood listing. 8. The input source listed (ICMR-NIN Dietary Guidelines PDF) supports menu context only; nutrient values are independently estimated from ICMR-NIN food composition tables and USDA FoodData Central.
